problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
hypothesis: The key to Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? is Monoclonal antibody blocking protein-protein interaction — specifically Monoclonal antibody blocking — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 20 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities. In the expert's mind there are few. — Shunryu Suzuki
